title: Keteleeria roulletii
taxonomy: Keteleeria roulletii is a species of coniferous tree within the family Pinaceae. It belongs to the genus Keteleeria, which is native to Southeast Asia. The genus Keteleeria is part of the family Pinaceae, which includes other coniferous trees like pines and spruces. The family Pinaceae is part of the larger group Pinopsida, which consists of coniferous trees. Keteleeria roulletii is classified as an evergreen tree and is a unique species within the plant kingdom.

intro: Keteleeria roulletii, also known as Roullet's fir, is a species of coniferous tree native to Vietnam and China. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Keteleeria roulletii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
